primary.note: Default source color #8a6048; the whole palette is derived from it at runtime. User-swappable across 14 palettes + custom.

surface.note: Cards, side rail, panels. One tonal step off Canvas. Dark mode: oklch(0.210 ...).

muted-ink.note: Secondary text: timestamps, counts, hints. Holds >=4.5:1 on Canvas. Dark mode: oklch(0.720 ...).

hairline.note: 1px borders and dividers; do the structural work shadows do elsewhere. Dark mode: low-alpha white.

alert.note: The one fixed, source-independent color. Destructive + error. Used mostly as alert/10 tinted fill.

success.note: Fixed, source-independent, paired with Alert (same lightness, matching green hue). Addition/staged-add state and `+N` diff line tints — never a solid block, low-alpha tints or Micro-text digits only. (The Changes-panel status letters + `+N`/`−N` row stats use the softer `--git-*` palette instead.)

git-added.note: Fixed, source-independent SOFT green (light oklch(0.62 0.1 158) / dark oklch(0.8 0.1 160)). Pierre git-status palette: the `A`/`U` status letters + `+N` stats in the Changes panel. Lower chroma than `--success` (which stays saturated for danger/diff tints app-wide); scoped to the status vocabulary.

git-modified.note: Fixed, source-independent SOFT blue (light oklch(0.6 0.11 248) / dark oklch(0.78 0.1 248)). Pierre git-status palette: the `M` status letter in the Changes panel.

git-renamed.note: Fixed, source-independent SOFT gold (light oklch(0.64 0.09 80) / dark oklch(0.84 0.08 82)). Pierre git-status palette: the `R` status letter in the Changes panel.

git-deleted.note: Fixed, source-independent SOFT red/coral (light oklch(0.6 0.13 25) / dark oklch(0.76 0.12 25)). Pierre git-status palette: the `D` status letter + `−N` stats in the Changes panel. Softer than `--destructive` (which stays loud for danger).

lane-1.note: Commit graph branch lane (hue = source). Distinguished by lightness + position + label, not hue alone. Chroma follows the source with a floor of 0.12 (max(c, 0.12)) so a grey source still yields distinguishable lanes. Runtime tokens --lane-1…5.

heading.purpose: Screen and section titles. The ceiling: no hero headings in a tool. Fixed rem, never clamp().

body.purpose: Commit messages, descriptions, prose. Cap real prose at 65-75ch.

label.purpose: The workhorse: buttons, field labels, list rows, the rail, branch names.

micro.purpose: Badges, +N/-N diff stats, xs buttons, ref pills. Smallest readable step.

mono.purpose: Code, diffs, and raw file content only (DM Mono, 13px, 400). The one sanctioned second family; never UI chrome.

shadows.name: overlay
shadows.purpose: True overlays only: popover, dropdown menu, command palette, dialog, drag preview. Tinted with Ink, never pure black. Nothing at rest.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: Default easing (ease-out-expo feel). No bounce, no elastic.

motion.name: duration-fast
motion.purpose: Most state transitions (hover, fills). Users are in flow.

breakpoints.name: compact
breakpoints.purpose: Below this, collapse the side rail to icons; structural, not fluid type.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The bold action (Push, Commit). Wears the user's source tint, not a fixed red.

components.name: Ghost Icon Button
components.description: Default for toolbar icon buttons. Transparent at rest, muted fill on hover.
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-ghost" aria-label="Search"><svg width="14" height="14" viewBox="0 0 256 256" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="18" stroke-linecap="round"><circle cx="116" cy="116" r="84"/><line x1="175" y1="175" x2="224" y2="224"/></svg></button>

components.name: Destructive Button
components.description: Subtle by default: tinted alert fill, never solid red. Solid red is reserved for the most dangerous confirmation only.

components.name: Input
components.description: Commit message / search field. Tinted fill, 1px hairline, button-matched height.

components.name: Input Group (flat search)
components.description: Field with a leading/trailing addon — the group owns the chrome, the inner input (Input ghost) stays borderless. The flat (ghost) variant has no border, fill, or halo: the shared search field for the Combobox popover and the rail's global filter.

components.name: Ref Pill
components.description: Branch / tag marker. Tiny, quiet, scannable. The active branch and Detached HEAD take a stronger tint.

components.name: Toast
components.description: Transient feedback / error surface, bottom-right. A true overlay: Surface fill, 1px Hairline, the one Overlay shadow. Color spent only on state — an alert glyph flags an error toast. Reserve for out-of-band notices, not routine confirmations.

Could not open repository
/Users/me/notes is not a git repository.

components.name: Change Row
components.description: Dense single-line file row in the Inspector's Changes panel (issue #62). Groups render Unstaged then Staged (Unstaged on top) and BOTH always render on a dirty tree — an empty group shows a quiet Muted-Ink hint with its bulk action disabled. Group headers share the rail's FULL section-header grammar (chevron + leading duotone icon + sentence-case label + mono count chip — dashed circle for Unstaged, check circle for Staged; never a bare all-caps eyebrow) on a sticky, opaque, borderless one-step Muted-Surface band that pins while its list scrolls, carrying the right-aligned Stage all / Unstage all text action (count chip drops to Canvas on the band). Like the rail's sections a group collapses from its header, open state persisted per group. GitKraken-style the Muted-Ink directory path sits on the left (truncating head-first) and the Body-size filename (medium weight) on the right, so the filename is what you scan (it ellipsizes only if it alone overruns the row). At the far right a single status letter (A/M/D/R/U) is always shown, tinted by kind in Pierre's SOFT git-status palette: blue modified, green add/untracked, gold renamed, red delete — the pastel --git-* tokens (CHANGE_LETTER_TONE; the +N/−N stats use the same green/red) — the same soft per-kind hue the graph's WIP-row summary chips wear, so a change kind reads identically everywhere it's counted. On hover/focus a right cluster fades in just left of the letter with the `+N`/`−N` Micro stats (a zero side omitted) and a single stage/unstage action (a `+` icon button when Unstaged, `−` when Staged), absolutely placed with a gradient fade so they never shift the filename — no persistent checkbox. A leading file-type icon is omitted for now (returns with @pierre/trees). Never boxed: separated by tone, not borders.
components.html: ✎apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/modules/changes/ChangeRow.tsx+24 −6

components.name: Commit Graph Row
components.description: Signature component. Colored lanes + node draw branches as a transit map; the row carries a barely-there tint of the lane color. The protagonist of the UI. The lanes zone caps at a max width (12 columns) and pans horizontally behind one shared quiet scrollbar so a wide graph never pushes the COMMIT column away. Lane lines rest at 80% opacity so the graph reads light. Every row fill is the row's own lane color, never a neutral: a four-step ladder (6% at rest, 16% for the armed run's members, 26% under the pointer so the hovered row reads as the anchor inside the lit run, 36% persistent for the selected row — in the graph, selection spends the lane's color instead of Accent Surface); selection outranks hover. Hovering a commit row (or a ref pill) highlights its branch run — stronger row tint, fully-opaque line at unchanged weight (the opacity spread carries the pop, no thickening) while other lines recede to 35% and other rows' subject+author dim to 50%, an undecorated row carries the Branch name of the run it rides as a ghost pill in the REFS zone, revealed instantly on the row's hover (ellipsized like any pill, faded to 60%, never covering the lanes; hovering the zone extends it full and solid; when no Ref names the line the name is recovered from git's conventional merge subject, a real Ref always winning). Ref pills ellipsize inside the REFS zone at rest (never hard-cropped mid-letter); hovering the zone itself (not the whole row, so sweeping the list never blankets the lanes) immediately extends them past the zone, floating over the lanes (opaque fills, GitKraken-familiar); the AUTHOR zone rests as just the relative time + avatar pinned at the row's right edge (the real GitHub profile photo when the commit publicly implies the account — noreply email, or an author name shaped like a GitHub username — ADR 0014, no Gravatar, emails never leave the machine; otherwise the identity-color initials, which also underlay the photo while it loads), and hovering or selecting the row reveals the author's name to the avatar's right, nudging the time + avatar left (the subject absorbs the squeeze). The WIP row's change summary (Changes-panel icons in the pastel --git-* tones + aggregate +N/−N) sits right beside its subject, not at the row's far edge. The highlight arms on hover intent (500ms rest); once armed, moving along the run's own rows hands the highlight over seamlessly (no reset, no re-arm delay), while moving anywhere else resets it immediately; it fades over 150ms (motion-safe only).

narrative.overview: gitoui is a craftsman's workbench, not a cockpit. A workbench is dense (every tool within reach) but never noisy: each tool has its place and the surface itself recedes so the work stays in focus. Here the work is your repository's history, and the commit graph is the protagonist. Everything around it (the repository rail, the staging panel, the sync actions) is the well-organized pegboard that frames the work without competing with it. The system is built on one unusual idea: the entire palette is tinted from a single source color the user chooses (default a worn-leather brown, #8a6048). Every surface, border, and text tone is derived from that seed in OKLCH at runtime, so the app feels like one continuous, personally-tinted material rather than a kit of unrelated greys. The default tint carries very little chroma, which is the point: the chrome is a quiet warm near-neutral, and saturated color is spent only where it earns its keep (primary actions, state, and the graph's lanes). It explicitly rejects GitKraken's saturated cockpit and generic SaaS.
keyCharacteristics: One continuous source-tinted material; chrome is a calm warm near-neutral.
The commit graph is the visual hero; chrome recedes around it.
Compact and precise: small, dense controls built for a tool, not a marketing page.
Flat by default; depth comes from tint and 1px hairlines, not shadow.
One sans (DM Sans) carries the whole interface through weight and size; a companion mono (DM Mono) is scoped strictly to code and diff content.
Light and dark are equal citizens, generated by the same formulas.

rules.name: The Living Tint Rule
rules.body: There are no hardcoded greys. Every surface, border, and text tone is oklch(from var(--primary-source) L calc(c * k) h), derived from the one source color. Never introduce a flat #888 or a Tailwind neutral-500; pull from the token or it will not re-tint.

rules.name: The Spent Color Rule
rules.body: Saturated color is a currency. Spend it on the primary action, the current selection, live state, the graph lanes, and identity avatars (author/repo). Everything else is tinted near-neutral. If an element is colorful and isn't one of those five, it's a leak.

rules.name: The Two-Family Rule
rules.body: DM Sans carries the entire interface: headings, body, labels, data, and buttons. DM Mono is the only sanctioned second family, and only for rendering real code, diffs, and file content (the documented exception this rule always reserved). No third family, no display serif, no decorative face: if text isn't code, it's DM Sans.

rules.name: The Fixed Scale Rule
rules.body: Sizes are a fixed rem scale, never clamp(). A tool is viewed at a consistent DPI; fluid headings that shrink inside a panel look broken, not responsive.

rules.name: The Flat-At-Rest Rule
rules.body: Cards, panels, the side rail, list rows, and the graph carry no shadow. If you reach for box-shadow on a resting surface, use a Hairline border or a tonal step instead.

rules.name: The No Ghost-Card Rule
rules.body: Never pair a 1px border with a wide soft drop shadow on the same element. Pick one: a single Hairline border, or (for a true overlay) the Overlay shadow. Never both.

rules.name: The Quiet-Scrollbar Rule
rules.body: Scrollbars are chrome and must recede: a thin (~4px), fully rounded thumb tinted from --muted-foreground (25% at rest, 40% on hover) floating in a transparent track, never the chunky opaque platform default. Defined once globally on ::-webkit-scrollbar* in globals.css; don't restyle scrollbars per component.

dos: Do derive every grey from the source token (oklch(from var(--primary-source) ...)). Pull from the palette so the UI re-tints.
Do keep chrome a calm near-neutral and spend saturated color only on the primary action, current selection, live state, the graph lanes, and identity avatars (author/repo).
Do keep surfaces flat at rest; build depth from the Canvas->Surface tonal step and 1px Hairlines. Shadows are for true overlays only.
Do carry DM Sans for all UI and build hierarchy with weight + size on a fixed rem scale; reserve DM Mono strictly for code / diff / file content.
Do keep controls compact (buttons 28px default) and the vocabulary identical screen to screen.
Do hold Muted Ink to >=4.5:1, and treat light and dark with equal craft across all palettes.
Do keep the graph readable by position + label, not hue alone.
donts: Don't rebuild GitKraken's saturated cockpit: no wall-to-wall panels, gradients, or heavy chrome competing with the graph.
Don't drift into generic SaaS: no rounded card grids, no all-caps tracked eyebrows over panels, no purple gradients, no gradient text, no hero-metric template.
Don't box list rows into cards, and never nest cards. Rows are separated by tone, not borders.
Don't pair a 1px border with a wide soft drop shadow (the ghost-card tell), and don't put any shadow on a resting surface.
Don't over-round: buttons/cards/inputs stay 4-11px; 24px+ radii are banned.
Don't introduce a flat hardcoded grey (#888, neutral-500), a third font family, or DM Mono anywhere but code / diff / file content.
Don't use clamp() / fluid type, hero-scale headings, or all-caps body copy in a tool.
Don't let the loud action be a fixed red; the primary action wears the user's source tint, red means destructive only.
